headline: Brand Asset Management Group Inc. Has $350,000 Holdings in Tesla, Inc. $TSLA
summary: Brand Asset Management Group Inc. significantly reduced its stake in Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) by 83.2% in the fourth quarter, now holding 778 shares valued at $350,000. This reduction comes amid other institutional investors adjusting their positions and recent insider selling, including transactions by Tesla's Director Kathleen Wilson-Thompson and CFO Vaibhav Taneja. Despite a 3% drop in share price, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average target price of $404.37, driven by mixed sentiments regarding Tesla's valuation and future prospects in autonomy and energy.

headline: [Form 4] Tesla, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
summary: Tesla's Chief Financial Officer, Vaibhav Taneja, engaged in insider trading activity, selling 2,605.5 shares of common stock valued at approximately $1.05 million. This sale was primarily to satisfy tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units. The Form 4 filing details the transactions and Taneja's remaining direct and indirect holdings in Tesla stock.

headline: A Nissan Leaf by Another Name: Mitsubishi Unveils 2027 Eclipse Sportback EV
summary: Mitsubishi has unveiled images of the 2027 Eclipse Sportback, an electric vehicle based on the redesigned 2026 Nissan Leaf. While technical details and pricing are not yet available, Mitsubishi confirmed cosmetic changes will differentiate it from the Leaf. The Eclipse Sportback is expected to go on sale by early fall 2026, marking Mitsubishi's return to the all-electric vehicle market since the i-MiEV was discontinued after the 2017 model year.

headline: Major auto parts maker open to assembling Chinese vehicles in Canada
summary: Magna International, a major global automotive supplier headquartered in Canada, is open to assembling Chinese vehicles in Canada if the plans are long-term. CEO Swamy Kotagiri emphasized that Magna aims to grow with Chinese automakers expanding globally, leveraged by their existing production and systemic knowledge from nearly two decades of operation in China. This strategy follows Magna's recent contract to build two models for Chinese EV maker XPENG in Austria and aligns with growing ties between Canadian companies and Chinese automakers due to shifting trade dynamics.

headline: Tesla Wins Denmark Green Light For FSD Expansion — But TSLA Stock Slips
summary: Tesla has received approval from the Danish Road Traffic Authority for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) driver assistance software to be used on public roads in Denmark. This expansion follows similar approvals in other European nations like the Netherlands, Lithuania, and Estonia, though EU-wide approval is still pending. Despite this progress in FSD expansion, Tesla's stock (TSLA) slipped 4%, and retail sentiment remained bearish, amid distractions and delays related to other company ventures.

headline: Tesla (TSLA) Rolls Out Unsupervised Robotaxis in Austin Metro Area, Reuters Reports
summary: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is expanding its unsupervised robotaxi service throughout the Austin Metro area, a move reported by Reuters in early June. This initiative is central to Tesla's growth strategy, which has pivoted towards AI and robotics, aiming to accelerate its autonomous ride-hailing operations. Additionally, Estonia's transport authority recently cleared Tesla's FSD driver-assistance system for use on its roads, though it remains a Level 2 system requiring driver responsibility.

headline: Yikes! Elon Musk's Robotaxi Fleet Is Smaller Than Pepsi's Doritos Truck Fleet
summary: Despite Elon Musk's emphasis on autonomy, PepsiCo has a larger fleet of fully driverless trucks operating on public roads than Tesla's unsupervised robotaxi service, according to a recent Wall Street Journal report. PepsiCo's 41 driverless trucks, used for hauling snacks and beverages, have logged zero accidents and high on-time rates, while Tesla's robotaxi fleet is significantly smaller and faces skepticism from prediction markets regarding its future deployment. Tesla's market capitalization relies heavily on its autonomy thesis, but meaningful robotaxi fleet expansion and FSD software release are delayed.

headline: Trump Media stock hits 52-week low at 3.8 USD
summary: Trump Media & Technology Group’s equity warrants (DJTWW) have fallen to a 52-week low of $3.82, marking a significant 72% decline from their peak of $13.48. The company's stock has also seen a substantial 69.84% drop over the past year, with a 9.5% decrease in the last week alone, reflecting ongoing market pressures and negative investor sentiment.

headline: With The SpaceX IPO Imminent, Nvidia And Tesla Accelerate The Race For Physical AI
summary: Nvidia and Tesla are leading the race in physical AI, pursuing different strategies to dominate the sector as the SpaceX IPO approaches. Nvidia focuses on being an "arms dealer" by providing platform infrastructure, partnering with hardware manufacturers globally, including China's Unitree Robotics. In contrast, Tesla under Elon Musk aims for vertical integration, building the entire AI stack for its Optimus robot, leveraging its existing FSD technology. This competition highlights the distinct approaches of the U.S. and China in developing embodied intelligence, with implications for labor economics, supply chain control, and national security, especially if a Tesla-SpaceX merger occurs.

headline: Solidion Strengthens Battery Moat with 7 New Patents
summary: Solidion Technology, Inc. announced it has been granted seven new U.S. patents for anode materials used in lithium batteries, bringing its total U.S. patents to 130. The company's anode technology aims to enhance AI data center uptime with higher energy density, lower costs, and domestically sourced materials. Solidion also raised $35 million through a stock sale to commercialize its Extreme-Climate Battery technology.

headline: McDonald’s Stock Could Be Bottoming, Creating a Contrarian Bet for Long-Term Investors
summary: McDonald's stock (MCD) appears to be establishing a technical floor around $275-$280 after a period of consolidation, despite a recent 10% decline. Technical indicators show signs of bottoming, with a bullish divergence in the percentage price oscillator. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, with an average price target of $333, suggesting a potential 20% upside for this defensive, low-beta stock.

headline: Brand Engagement Network forms AI health joint venture with Intervent International
summary: Brand Engagement Network (NASDAQ:BNAI) has established Intervent Health AI, Inc., a 50/50 joint venture with Intervent International, LLC. This new entity will focus on developing and commercializing AI-powered health coaching solutions, combining Brand Engagement Network’s conversational AI with Intervent’s clinical methodologies. The venture includes exclusive commercialization agreements for North America through SKYE AI USA, LLC, and non-exclusive reseller arrangements internationally, with both founding companies holding equal ownership stakes.

headline: What Happens If Tesla and SpaceX Merge?
summary: Morningstar analysts examine the potential for a merger between Tesla and SpaceX, driven by Elon Musk's desire for consolidation and increasing AI collaboration between the companies. While a merger presents strong business cases like streamlined operations and shared AI development, significant hurdles exist, including divergent long-term goals, regulatory concerns, and especially the challenge of agreeing on valuation and equity distribution that satisfies both Tesla and SpaceX shareholders, given SpaceX's high valuation multiples. They expect discussions to intensify after SpaceX's IPO.
source: Seth Goldstein, CFA and Nicolas Owens

headline: Wolfspeed’s new chip tech designed to boost EV range and charging efficiency
summary: Wolfspeed has introduced its fifth generation of Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFET technology, which significantly improves efficiency for 1200 V and 750 V automotive and industrial applications. This new Gen 5 technology, built on a 200 mm manufacturing platform, reduces specific on-resistance by up to 27%, leading to increased EV mileage range, better charging infrastructure efficiency, and more compact system designs. The technology also enhances durability with an improved junction temperature and provides a low-risk path to market for customers.

headline: Tesla, Inc. $TSLA Shares Acquired by Focus Partners Advisor Solutions LLC
summary: Focus Partners Advisor Solutions LLC significantly increased its stake in Tesla ($TSLA) by 10.1% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to $23.22 million. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the electric vehicle producer. The article also highlights recent positive and neutral news surrounding Tesla, including analyst ratings, price targets, and insider trading activities.

headline: Erste Asset Management GmbH Buys 26,829 Shares of Tesla, Inc. $TSLA
summary: Erste Asset Management GmbH significantly increased its stake in Tesla, Inc. by 205.5% in the fourth quarter, acquiring an additional 26,829 shares to hold a total of 39,883 shares valued at $18.3 million. This comes amidst other institutional investors also adjusting their positions in the electric vehicle producer. Various analysts have issued updated ratings and price objectives for Tesla, reflecting a mixed sentiment with positive notes on its "physical AI" potential and improving China sales, alongside concerns about its elevated valuation.

headline: Can Lemonade’s Indiana Tesla Discount Reveal the Real Scale of Its AI Edge (LMND)?
summary: Lemonade, Inc. has introduced an Autonomous Car insurance discount for Tesla owners in Indiana, offering 50% off miles driven using Full Self-Driving (Supervised). This move underscores Lemonade's AI-driven pricing and product strategy, which is critical for its long-term profitability. Investors are watching to see if this and other product rollouts contribute to improved loss ratios, capital discipline, and ultimately, a path to better earnings amidst ongoing losses and regulatory risks.

headline: Tesla Stock Forecast | SpaceX Listing, Q1 Results
summary: Tesla's stock performance and future outlook are influenced by several factors, including the upcoming SpaceX IPO, Tesla's Q1 2026 results which showed negative free cash flow due to increased AI and robotaxi infrastructure spending, and varying analyst price targets. Analysts are split on whether the SpaceX listing will benefit or detract from Tesla's capital flows, while the company's shift towards AI, autonomous driving, and robotics continues to be a key focus for investors and analysts. Despite a recent recovery, the stock remains below its December 2025 all-time high, with consensus price targets ranging from $404.37 to $419.94.

headline: TSLA Stock: Cathie Wood Slaps $75 Robotaxi Ticket Into ARK’s Bull Case
summary: Cathie Wood of ARK Invest experienced an unsupervised Tesla Robotaxi ride in Austin, during which the vehicle received a $75 parking ticket. This incident led her to add a new line item for tickets to ARK's Tesla valuation model, though she maintains a bullish stance on Tesla's robotaxi potential, projecting it to drive a significant portion of the company's enterprise value. Despite ARK's optimism, Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system faces ongoing investigation by the NHTSA for safety concerns and has been linked to several reportable incidents involving its Austin robotaxi fleet.

headline: Vaibhav Taneja files Form 144 to sell 3,000 TSLA shares (TSLA)
summary: Vaibhav Taneja has filed a Form 144 to sell 3,000 shares of Tesla (TSLA) common stock. The proposed sale, which is for restricted stock, has a transaction date of May 13, 2026, and is expected to generate $1,350,000.00. A Form 144 is a notice of intent to sell restricted securities and does not confirm the actual sale has occurred.

headline: JPMorgan Just Dramatically Reversed Course on Tesla Because TSLA Stock Has Massive Physical AI Potential
summary: JPMorgan, a long-time skeptic of Tesla, has dramatically upgraded TSLA stock to "Neutral" from "Underweight" and increased its price target to $475 from $145. This reversal is primarily due to Tesla's leading position in "physical AI," encompassing self-driving cars, factory robots, and the Optimus humanoid robot. The bank highlights Tesla's manufacturing scale, vertical integration, and rapid technology development as unmatched strengths, although it acknowledges that near-term valuations are still high and significant returns are expected later in the decade.

headline: Honda Motor (TSE:7267) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Rebound
summary: Honda Motor (TSE:7267) has seen a 13% share price increase over the last month, but is still down 2% over three months. While a popular valuation narrative suggests the stock is 5.6% undervalued with a fair value of ¥1,523.50 based on earnings recovery and electrification spending, an alternative discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates a fair value of ¥936.45, suggesting it might be overvalued. Investors are encouraged to conduct independent research to weigh these differing valuation signals.

headline: Video: 2026 Hyundai Palisade Hybrid Vs. Toyota Grand Highlander Hybrid: Which 3-Row SUV Is King?
summary: This article features a video comparing the 2026 Hyundai Palisade Hybrid and the Toyota Grand Highlander Hybrid. Both are highly anticipated, $60,000 three-row hybrid SUVs, and the video puts them head-to-head to determine which one reigns supreme. The video has a runtime of 6 minutes and 1 second.

headline: Rocket Lab stock rises on record revenue and sector volatility
summary: Rocket Lab USA Inc. (RKLB) stock increased by 2.47% to $112.80, driven by traders buying the dip amidst volatility in the aerospace and space defense sectors. The company reported record first-quarter revenue of $200.35 million, surpassing analyst expectations by 63.5% year-over-year, and projects Q2 revenue between $225 million and $240 million. This performance highlights Rocket Lab's operational consistency, especially after competitor Blue Origin's rocket failure and SpaceX's trimmed IPO valuation, positioning Rocket Lab favorably in the market.

headline: ASML Holding NV Stock (ASML) Moved Up by 7.15% on Jun 8: Key Drivers Unveiled
summary: ASML Holding NV (ASML) stock surged 7.15% on June 8, driven by Elon Musk's endorsement and positive analyst revisions. Musk's mention of ASML as "arguably the greatest company in Europe" and its essential role in SpaceX and Tesla's Terafab project, which will rely on ASML's EUV lithography technology, significantly boosted market sentiment. Additionally, leading firms like BofA, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley raised price targets, maintaining "Buy" or "Overweight" recommendations, reflecting confidence in ASML's future prospects, strong Q1 2026 results, and increased 2026 revenue outlook due to high AI-driven demand.

headline: Tesla Inc Stock (TSLA) Moved Up by 3.38% on Jun 8: Drivers Behind the Movement
summary: Tesla Inc. (TSLA) stock rose by 3.38% on June 8, driven by advances in autonomous driving, an analyst upgrade from JPMorgan, and improved market share. The company began rolling out its robotaxi service in Austin, expanded its Full Self-Driving software, and reclaimed the top spot in the global EV market share for Q1 2026. However, concerns about Cybertruck demand, intense competition, and high valuation persist.

headline: Apple Is 'Famously Late To New Markets': Why Gene Munster Says 2026 Is 'The Most Important WWDC' In 43 Years
summary: Tech investor Gene Munster believes the 2026 Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) will be Apple's most crucial in 43 years, as the company needs to demonstrate strong AI leadership. While Apple has historically been slow to enter new markets, Munster highlights the company's consistent customer loyalty and strong iPhone sales growth tied to anticipated AI upgrades. He expects significant announcements around "New Siri," iOS 27, and macOS 27, aiming to convince investors that Apple can deliver compelling and secure personalized AI features.

headline: Google reportedly orders 3 million AI chips from Intel for 2028 production
summary: Google has reportedly placed an order for over three million tensor processing units from Intel, intended for production in 2028. This move comes as Nvidia is also evaluating Intel's technology for a processor combining four graphics chips. Intel's shares saw a significant rise following this news, and Tesla CEO Elon Musk previously stated plans to use Intel's 14A manufacturing process for his Terafab project.

headline: Optimism For General Motors Shares Contains A Flaw: The United Auto Workers Union (GM)
summary: General Motors (GM) faces a Hold rating due to significant labor risks associated with the United Auto Workers (UAW) union and its dependence on U.S. pickup truck profitability. Recent share outperformance has been bolstered by a slowdown in battery electric vehicle (BEV) production and substantial write-offs. However, an ongoing UAW strike at Dauch Corp. highlights the vulnerability of GM's high-margin pickup production, and the company's long-term reliance on the UAW and shrinking U.S. market share against non-unionized competitors limit its upside potential.

headline: Electrovaya wins huge price target raise from this analyst
summary: Roth Capital Partners analyst Craig Irwin has raised his price target for Electrovaya (NASDAQ:ELVA) to $20.00 from $12.00, maintaining a "Buy" rating. This update follows a visit to the company's New York plant, where Irwin found Electrovaya on track to ramp up production, with demand potentially leading to a sold-out facility by the end of 2026. The increased target reflects Electrovaya's differentiated technology, strong demand, and clear production ramp visibility, with significant opportunities in energy storage and niobium oxide cells.

headline: SunCar Wins Insurance Management Contract for Aistaland, a Luxury EV Brand Backed by Huawei
summary: SunCar Technology Group Inc. has secured a contract to manage auto insurance for Aistaland, a luxury EV brand co-developed by Huawei, making SunCar the exclusive insurance partner for all Huawei luxury EV partner brands. SunCar will provide customized AI-powered insurance solutions, leveraging its extensive vehicle database and large language models to calculate risk, match policies, predict maintenance, and reduce claims costs for Aistaland owners. This partnership solidifies SunCar's position within the Huawei ecosystem and expands its presence in the luxury EV market in China.

headline: Huawei-backed luxury EVs pick SunCar to power AI insurance
summary: SunCar Technology (NASDAQ: SDA) has secured a contract to manage auto insurance for Aistaland, a luxury EV brand co-developed by Huawei, becoming the exclusive insurance partner for all Huawei luxury EV partner brands. The partnership leverages SunCar’s AI models, Doubao’s large language model, and a network of 48,000 service providers to offer integrated insurance and automotive services within Huawei’s Qiankun intelligent vehicle system. This agreement expands SunCar’s portfolio, which already includes partnerships with over 20 EV brands, and solidifies its position in the luxury EV market within the Huawei ecosystem.

headline: Mazda Motor Settles on PTC Inc. (PTC)’s Codebeamer for Software-Defined Vehicle Development
summary: Mazda Motor Corporation has selected PTC Inc.'s Codebeamer Application Lifecycle Management solution to support its software-defined vehicle development (SDV). This integration will standardize requirements testing and validation processes, enhance traceability, and improve collaboration across teams. The partnership aligns with PTC’s vision for the Intelligent Product Lifecycle, providing a digital backbone for industrial software solutions in robotics and automation.

headline: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods Downgrades Blackstone Secured Lending Fund to Market Perform From Outperform, Adjusts Price Target to $24 From $25
summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods has downgraded Blackstone Secured Lending Fund (BXSL) from an "Outperform" rating to "Market Perform." Concurrently, the firm adjusted its price target for BXSL, lowering it from $25 to $24. This change reflects a revised outlook on the investment company.

headline: Manchester Capital Management LLC Has $8.30 Million Stock Holdings in Tesla, Inc. $TSLA
summary: Manchester Capital Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Tesla, Inc. by 52.6% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 18,449 shares valued at $8.30 million. This investment now represents 1.1% of the firm's portfolio. Despite notable insider selling and a consensus "Hold" rating from Wall Street, analysts are considering Tesla's potential in AI, robotics, and robotaxis as long-term growth drivers.

headline: Hantz Financial Services Inc. Sells 20,223 Shares of Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
summary: Hantz Financial Services Inc. reduced its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. by 4.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 20,223 shares and maintaining 419,277 shares valued at approximately $96.8 million. Despite recent insider selling by Amazon's CEOs, the company continues to receive a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with a target price of $312.52, following strong earnings that surpassed expectations. Amazon's continued investments in AI, logistics, and cloud services (AWS) further bolster its long-term outlook, though regulatory and cost concerns persist.

headline: Glenview Trust Co Has $146.22 Million Stake in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
summary: Glenview Trust Co increased its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. by 2.2% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 633,493 shares valued at approximately $146.2 million. Amazon's stock is benefiting from AI and cloud growth, including a new warehouse robot and a significant cloud services deal with Pinterest. However, the company faces challenges such as insider selling, scrutiny over AI spending amidst layoffs, and rising shipping costs that could affect retail margins.

headline: Google, Nvidia consider Intel as backup chip manufacturer, The Information reports
summary: Alphabet's Google has reportedly placed an order with Intel to manufacture over three million tensor processing units by 2028. Nvidia is also evaluating Intel's technology for a processor combining four graphics chips. This news indicates a potential turnaround for Intel in chip manufacturing, with Tesla CEO Elon Musk also previously mentioning plans to use Intel's 14A manufacturing process for his company's AI chip complex.

headline: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S Sells 21,660 Shares of O'Reilly Automotive, Inc. $ORLY
summary: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S reduced its stake in O'Reilly Automotive, Inc. by 28% in the fourth quarter, selling 21,660 shares and retaining 55,732 shares valued at approximately $5.08 million. Despite this reduction, institutional ownership in ORLY remains high at 85%. The company recently beat quarterly earnings expectations, reporting $0.72 EPS on $4.56 billion in revenue, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $112.25.

headline: Autonomous & Self-Driving Vehicle News: WeRide, Uber, Zoox, Volvo Nuro, Lucid & Tesla.
summary: This article provides updates on various autonomous and self-driving vehicle initiatives involving companies like WeRide, Uber, Zoox, Volvo Autonomous Solutions, Nuro, Lucid, and Tesla. Key developments include WeRide and Uber launching a robotaxi pilot in Madrid, Lockton and Nexar's new framework for AV risk assessment, and Uber's significant investment in Nuro for an autonomous Lucid vehicle fleet. Tesla is also highlighted for facing legal challenges regarding retroactive modifications to FSD purchase agreements.

headline: Onvo Begins Gen-3 L60 Test Drives Ahead of June 11 Launch
summary: Onvo has initiated public test drives for its third-generation L60 SUV across China in anticipation of its official launch on June 11. This new model will feature significant hardware upgrades, including a LiDAR sensor and the in-house Shenji NX9031 smart-driving chip, leading to an expected price increase. The move follows Nio's successful strategy of early showroom placement and pre-launch test drives to generate momentum for new models.

headline: The Next Force In Luxury Cars Doesn't Come From Germany Or Korea
summary: Chinese luxury car brands like Nio, Hongqi, Zeekr, Denza, and Yangwang are rapidly redefining the global luxury automotive market with advanced technology, innovative designs, and high-performance vehicles. These brands are gaining traction in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia, proving their worth in the domestic market before expanding internationally. While current US policies limit their entry, a Cox Automotive study indicates significant consumer interest, especially among Gen Z, for considering Chinese car brands.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Foxconn's Rival, BYD's Sales Surge And Tesla's Potential Win In Canada
summary: This article summarizes a busy week in the EV industry, highlighting Foxconn's new Cavira EV challenging Tesla's Model Y with greater range and interior space. It also covers BYD's surge in overseas sales, breaking a slump, and discusses Tesla's potential benefits from a Canada-China EV deal that could bring more Chinese-made EVs to Canada.

headline: WINTON GROUP Ltd Acquires New Stake in Tesla, Inc. $TSLA
summary: WINTON GROUP Ltd has acquired a new stake in Tesla, Inc. by purchasing 11,934 shares valued at approximately $5.37 million in the fourth quarter. This move highlights continuing heavy institutional interest in Tesla, with 66.2% of the stock now held by institutional investors and hedge funds, despite some insider selling and a split consensus among analysts who currently rate the stock as "Hold" with an average target price of $404.37.

headline: Vise Technologies Inc. Buys 6,602 Shares of Waste Management, Inc. $WM
summary: Vise Technologies Inc. significantly increased its stake in Waste Management (NYSE:WM) during the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 6,602 shares, bringing its total holdings to $3.25 million. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for WM with an average price target of $255.67, following strong earnings and dividend announcements. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Waste Management during the recent quarters.

headline: 2026.8.300 Official Tesla Release Notes - Software Updates
summary: Tesla has released update 2026.8.300, which includes several new features and improvements. Key updates include Dashcam Viewer improvements, visibility of the selected driver profile in the Tesla app, support for the Slovak language, and various enhancements to Service Mode. The update is currently rolling out to a small percentage of the fleet.

headline: Fjarde AP Fonden Fourth Swedish National Pension Fund Reduces Holdings in Church & Dwight Co., Inc. $CHD
summary: Fjarde AP Fonden Fourth Swedish National Pension Fund reduced its stake in Church & Dwight Co., Inc. by 6.3% in the fourth quarter, selling 11,400 shares but still owning 168,193 shares valued at approximately $14.1 million. This reduction comes amid mixed insider activity and analyst ratings, with the company reporting solid quarterly earnings and paying a regular dividend. Despite the fund's reduction, other institutional investors have recently increased their holdings in Church & Dwight.

headline: Capital World Investors Boosts Stake in General Motors Company $GM
summary: Capital World Investors increased its holdings in General Motors Company by 7.8% in the fourth quarter, now owning over 19.5 million shares worth approximately $1.59 billion. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions, while some company insiders recently sold shares. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for GM with an average target price of $95.65.

headline: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S Has $7.01 Million Position in Boyd Gaming Corporation $BYD
summary: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S reduced its stake in Boyd Gaming Corporation by 26.3% in the fourth quarter, holding 82,247 shares valued at $7.01 million. Despite insider selling by the CEO and Chairman, Boyd Gaming announced a $500 million stock buyback program and a quarterly dividend of $0.20 per share. The company currently has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $92.08.

headline: Daiwa Securities Group Inc. Sells 3,523 Shares of Woodward, Inc. $WWD
summary: Daiwa Securities Group Inc. reduced its stake in Woodward, Inc. by 8.7% in the fourth quarter, selling 3,523 shares and owning 36,819 shares worth $11.1 million. This comes amidst continued insider selling by key executives. Despite the institutional selling, Woodward reported strong quarterly results, surpassing EPS and revenue estimates, and declared a $0.32 per share dividend.
